data.content:
### PUT /orgs/{org}/teams/{team_slug}/repos/{owner}/{repo}

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/data/reusables/organizations/team-api.md

Add or update a team's repository permission. You can add a repository to a team or update the team's permission level for an existing repository.

```APIDOC
## PUT /orgs/{org}/teams/{team_slug}/repos/{owner}/{repo}

### Description
Adds a repository to a team or updates the team's permission for a repository.

### Method
PUT

### Endpoint
`/orgs/{org}/teams/{team_slug}/repos/{owner}/{repo}`

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **org** (string) - Required - The organization's name.
- **team_slug** (string) - Required - The URL-safe version of the team's name.
- **owner** (string) - Required - The owner of the repository (organization or user).
- **repo** (string) - Required - The name of the repository.

#### Request Body
- **permission** (string) - Required - The permission level to add or update. Accepted values are `pull` (Read) and `push` (Write).

### Request Example
```json
{
  "example": "PUT /orgs/my-org/teams/my-team/repos/my-org/my-repo"
}
```
```json
{
  "example": "{
    \"permission\": \"push\"
  }"
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (204)
No content. Indicates the operation was successful.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "example": ""
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Repository Permissions API

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/authentication/keeping-your-account-and-data-secure/managing-your-personal-access-tokens.md

Defines the available permission levels for repositories, applicable to both user and organization resource owners.

```APIDOC
## Repository Permissions

Repository permissions work for both user and organization resource owners.

### Available Permissions

- **actions** (read, write) - Actions
- **administration** (read, write) - Administration
- **artifact_metadata** (read, write) - Artifact Metadata
- **attestations** (read, write) - Attestations
- **security_events** (read, write) - Code scanning alerts
- **codespaces** (read, write) - Codespaces
- **codespaces_lifecycle_admin** (read, write) - Codespaces lifecycle admin
- **codespaces_metadata** (read) - Codespaces metadata
- **codespaces_secrets** (write) - Codespaces secrets
- **statuses** (read, write) - Commit statuses
- **contents** (read, write) - Contents
- **repository_custom_properties** (read, write) - Custom properties
- **vulnerability_alerts** (read, write) - Dependabot alerts
- **dependabot_secrets** (read, write) - Dependabot secrets
- **deployments** (read, write) - Deployments
- **discussions** (read, write) - Discussions
- **environments** (read, write) - Environments
- **issues** (read, write) - Issues
- **merge_queues** (read, write) - Merge queues
- **metadata** (read) - Metadata
- **pages** (read, write) - Pages
- **pull_requests** (read, write) - Pull requests
- **repository_advisories** (read, write) - Repository security advisories
- **secret_scanning_alerts** (read, write) - Secret scanning alerts
- **secrets** (read, write) - Secrets
- **actions_variables** (read, write) - Variables
- **repository_hooks** (read, write) - Webhooks
- **workflows** (write) - Workflows
```

--------------------------------

### About repository invitations

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/rest/collaborators/invitations.md

This section provides an overview of the repository invitations API. It explains how to view and manage invitations to collaborate on a repository. Invited users can accept or decline these invitations. It also clarifies the difference between adding a collaborator and inviting one, and details the required OAuth scopes.

```APIDOC
## About repository invitations

You can view and manage invitations to collaborate on a repository. The invited users (or external services on behalf of invited users) can choose to accept or decline the invitations.

To add a user as a collaborator, use the Collaborators endpoints instead. For more information, see [AUTOTITLE](/rest/collaborators/collaborators#add-a-repository-collaborator).

Note that the `repo:invite` [OAuth scope](/apps/oauth-apps/building-oauth-apps/scopes-for-oauth-apps) grants targeted access to invitations **without** also granting access to repository code, while the `repo` scope grants permission to code as well as invitations.
```

--------------------------------

### Add Guest Collaborator Role to Azure AD App Manifest

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/admin/managing-accounts-and-repositories/managing-users-in-your-enterprise/enabling-guest-collaborators.md

This JSON snippet shows how to add the 'Guest Collaborator' role to the appRoles section of your Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) application manifest. This enables the role to be assigned to users for guest collaboration in your enterprise. Ensure the 'id' matches the one provided in the documentation to avoid errors.

```json
{
  "allowedMemberTypes": [
    "User"
  ],
  "description": "Guest Collaborator",
  "displayName": "Guest Collaborator",
  "id": "1ebc4a02-e56c-43a6-92a5-02ee09b90824",
  "isEnabled": true,
  "lang": null,
  "origin": "Application",
  "value": null
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Codespaces Repository Permissions in devcontainer.json

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/codespaces/managing-your-codespaces/managing-repository-access-for-your-codespaces.md

This snippet shows how to define specific repository permissions within the `devcontainer.json` file for GitHub Codespaces. It allows granular control over access levels like 'write' for 'issues'. Permissions are applied to new codespaces after changes are committed.

```json
{
  "customizations": {
    "codespaces": {
      "repositories": {
        "my_org/my_repo": {
          "permissions": {
            "issues": "write"
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Add a repository to a user secret

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/rest/codespaces/secrets.md

Adds a repository to a user secret. This secret is made available to the codespace at runtime.

```APIDOC
## PUT /user/codespaces/secrets/{secret_name}/repositories/{repository_id}

### Description
Adds a repository to a user secret. This secret is made available to the codespace at runtime.

### Method
PUT

### Endpoint
/user/codespaces/secrets/{secret_name}/repositories/{repository_id}

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **secret_name** (string) - Required - The name of the secret.
- **repository_id** (integer) - Required - The ID of the repository to add.

### Response
#### Success Response (204)
No content is returned upon successful addition.

#### Error Response (422)
- **message** (string) - The error message.
- **errors** (array) - An array of error objects.
  - **resource** (string) - The resource that caused the error.
  - **field** (string) - The field that caused the error.
  - **code** (string) - The error code.

#### Error Response Example
```json
{
  "message": "Validation failed, and the endpoint could not be modernized.",
  "errors": [
    {
      "resource": "SecretRepository",
      "field": "repository_id",
      "code": "invalid"
    }
  ]
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Grant All Permissions to a Repository for Codespaces

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/codespaces/managing-your-codespaces/managing-repository-access-for-your-codespaces.md

This example demonstrates how to grant all read or write permissions to a specific repository for GitHub Codespaces using a shorthand notation in `devcontainer.json`. This simplifies the configuration when broad access is required.

```json
{
  "customizations": {
    "codespaces": {
      "repositories": {
        "my_org/my_repo": {
          "permissions": "write-all"
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Add Repository for Export using ghe-migrator CLI

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/migrations/using-ghe-migrator/exporting-migration-data-from-github-enterprise-server.md

This command adds a single repository to the migration export. It requires the repository URL and can optionally include `--lock` to lock the repository during the export process or `--exclude_attachments` to omit file attachments. The output includes a Migration GUID for future reference.

```shell
ghe-migrator add https://HOSTNAME/USERNAME/REPO-NAME --lock
ghe-migrator add https://HOSTNAME/USERNAME/REPO-NAME --exclude_attachments
ghe-migrator add -i PATH/TO/YOUR/REPOSITORY_URL.txt
```

--------------------------------

### Add Assignees to an Existing GitHub Issue using GitHub CLI

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/copilot/how-tos/use-copilot-agents/coding-agent/create-a-pr.md

This command uses the GitHub CLI to add assignees to an existing issue. It requires the repository owner, repository name, and issue number. The payload includes the assignees and optional agent assignment details.

```shell
gh api \
  --method POST \
  -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \
  -H "X-GitHub-Api-Version: 2022-11-28" \
  /repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/ISSUE_NUMBER/assignees \
  --input - <<< '{
  "assignees": ["copilot-swe-agent[bot]"],
  "agent_assignment": {
    "target_repo": "OWNER/REPO",
    "base_branch": "main",
    "custom_instructions": "",
    "custom_agent": "",
    "model": ""
  }
}'
```

--------------------------------

### Add Repository to Existing Export using ghe-migrator CLI

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/migrations/using-ghe-migrator/exporting-migration-data-from-github-enterprise-server.md

This command allows adding another repository to an ongoing export identified by a Migration GUID. It takes the new repository URL and the existing Migration GUID as parameters, along with an optional `--lock` flag.

```shell
ghe-migrator add https://HOSTNAME/USERNAME/OTHER-REPO-NAME -g MIGRATION-GUID --lock
```

--------------------------------

### Add Remote Repository Origin

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/pages/setting-up-a-github-pages-site-with-jekyll/creating-a-github-pages-site-with-jekyll.md

Adds a remote connection to the GitHub repository. This command specifies the URL of the remote repository, aliased as 'origin'.

```shell
git remote add origin https://github.com/USER/REPOSITORY.git
```

```shell
git remote add origin https://HOSTNAME/USER/REPOSITORY.git
```

--------------------------------

### Set Repository Interaction Restrictions

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/rest/interactions/repos.md

Temporarily restrict who can comment, open issues, or create pull requests in a public repository. This endpoint is equivalent to the {% data variables.product.repo-interactions-settings-page %} page.

```APIDOC
## PUT /repos/{owner}/{repo}/interaction-limits

### Description

Sets or updates the interaction restriction settings for a specific repository.

### Method

PUT

### Endpoint

`/repos/{owner}/{repo}/interaction-limits`

### Parameters

#### Path Parameters

- **owner** (string) - Required - The account owner of the repository.
- **repo** (string) - Required - The name of the repository.

#### Request Body

- **limit** (string) - Required - The interaction limit type ('one_day', 'one_week', 'one_month', 'three_months', 'six_months').

### Request Example

```json
{
  "example": "PUT /repos/octocat/Spoon-Knife/interaction-limits"
}
```

```json
{
  "example": {
    "limit": "three_months"
  }
}
```

### Response

#### Success Response (200)

- **limit** (string) - The interaction limit type (e.g., 'one_day', 'one_week', 'one_month', 'three_months', 'six_months').
- **origin** (string) - The source of the interaction limit (e.g., 'user', 'org', 'repository').
- **created_at** (string) - The timestamp when the interaction limit was created.
- **updated_at** (string) - The timestamp when the interaction limit was last updated.

#### Response Example

```json
{
  "example": {
    "limit": "three_months",
    "origin": "repository",
    "created_at": "2023-10-27T10:00:00Z",
    "updated_at": "2023-10-27T11:30:00Z"
  }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Make Shell Script Executable with Git

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/actions/tutorials/use-containerized-services/create-a-docker-container-action.md

These Git commands are used to add the shell script to the Git repository and explicitly set its execute permission. This ensures that the script can be run when the action is executed. The `git update-index --chmod=+x` command is particularly important for maintaining executable permissions across different systems.

```shell
git add entrypoint.sh
git update-index --chmod=+x entrypoint.sh
```

--------------------------------

### Remove Repository Interaction Restrictions

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/rest/interactions/repos.md

Stop restricting who can comment, open issues, or create pull requests in a public repository. This endpoint is equivalent to the {% data variables.product.repo-interactions-settings-page %} page.

```APIDOC
## DELETE /repos/{owner}/{repo}/interaction-limits

### Description

Removes all interaction restrictions for a specific repository.

### Method

DELETE

### Endpoint

`/repos/{owner}/{repo}/interaction-limits`

### Parameters

#### Path Parameters

- **owner** (string) - Required - The account owner of the repository.
- **repo** (string) - Required - The name of the repository.

### Request Example

```json
{
  "example": "DELETE /repos/octocat/Spoon-Knife/interaction-limits"
}
```

### Response

#### Success Response (204)

- No content is returned on successful deletion.

#### Response Example

```json
{
  "example": ""
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Add an upstream remote for a forked repository

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/get-started/using-git/pushing-commits-to-a-remote-repository.md

This command adds a new remote, typically named 'upstream', to your local Git clone. This allows you to fetch updates from the original repository when working with forks.

```shell
git remote add upstream THEIR_REMOTE_URL
```

--------------------------------

### Add upstream Git remote for a fork

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/pull-requests/collaborating-with-pull-requests/working-with-forks/configuring-a-remote-repository-for-a-fork.md

This command adds a new remote repository, typically named 'upstream', to your local Git configuration. It points to the original repository from which the fork was created. This remote is used for fetching updates from the original repository.

```shell
git remote add upstream https://{% data variables.product.product_url %}/ORIGINAL-OWNER/ORIGINAL-REPOSITORY.git
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Permissions for Binary Attestation

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/actions/how-tos/secure-your-work/use-artifact-attestations/use-artifact-attestations.md

Sets the necessary permissions in a GitHub Actions workflow to allow the generation of artifact attestations for binary files. Requires `id-token: write`, `contents: read`, and `attestations: write`.

```yaml
permissions:
  id-token: write
  contents: read
  attestations: write
```

--------------------------------

### Add New Remote Repository

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/get-started/using-git/splitting-a-subfolder-out-into-a-new-repository.md

Adds a new remote origin to your local repository, pointing to the newly created repository on GitHub. This allows you to push your filtered subfolder content to the new location. Common remote names like 'origin' or 'upstream' can be used.

```shell
git remote add origin https://{% data variables.product.product_url %}/USERNAME/REPOSITORY-NAME.git
```

--------------------------------

### Add Remote Repository URL using Git

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/migrations/importing-source-code/using-the-command-line-to-import-source-code/adding-locally-hosted-code-to-github.md

This command adds a remote origin to your local Git repository, linking it to a GitHub repository. You need to replace 'REMOTE-URL' with the actual URL of your GitHub repository. This is a crucial step for pushing local changes to GitHub.

```shell
git remote add origin REMOTE-URL
```

--------------------------------

### Add Copilot as an assignee using `addAssigneesToAssignable` mutation (shell)

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/copilot/how-tos/use-copilot-agents/coding-agent/create-a-pr.md

This shell command demonstrates how to use the `addAssigneesToAssignable` mutation to add Copilot to an issue without removing existing assignees. It requires the issue ID and bot ID, and supports the `agentAssignment` object for task customization. This is useful for collaborative assignment scenarios.

```shell
gh api graphql -f query='mutation {
  addAssigneesToAssignable(input: {
    assignableId: "ISSUE_ID",
    assigneeIds: ["BOT_ID"],
    agentAssignment: {
      targetRepositoryId: "REPOSITORY_ID",
      baseRef: "main",
      customInstructions: "Collaborate on this task",
      customAgent: "",
      model: ""
    }
  }) {
    assignable {
      ... on Issue {
        id
        title
        assignees(first: 10) {
          nodes {
            login
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}' -H 'GraphQL-Features: issues_copilot_assignment_api_support,coding_agent_model_selection'
```

--------------------------------

### Add a remote repository using Git

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/get-started/git-basics/managing-remote-repositories.md

Adds a new remote repository to your local Git configuration. It requires a remote name (e.g., 'origin') and a remote URL. Use 'git remote -v' to verify the addition.

```shell
$ git remote add origin https://{% data variables.product.product_url %}/OWNER/REPOSITORY.git
# Set a new remote

$ git remote -v
# Verify new remote
> origin  https://{% data variables.product.product_url %}/OWNER/REPOSITORY.git (fetch)
> origin  https://{% data variables.product.product_url %}/OWNER/REPOSITORY.git (push)
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Permissions for Container Image Attestation

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/actions/how-tos/secure-your-work/use-artifact-attestations/use-artifact-attestations.md

Configures required permissions in a GitHub Actions workflow for generating artifact attestations for container images. Includes `id-token: write`, `contents: read`, `attestations: write`, and `packages: write`.

```yaml
permissions:
  id-token: write
  contents: read
  attestations: write
  packages: write
```

--------------------------------

### Dependabot Secrets API

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/rest/dependabot/secrets.md

You can create, update, delete, and retrieve information about encrypted secrets using the REST API. Authenticated users must have collaborator access to a repository to create, update, or read secrets. GitHub Apps must have the `dependabot_secrets` permission to use these endpoints.

```APIDOC
## About Dependabot Secrets

You can create, update, delete, and retrieve information about encrypted secrets using the REST API. For more information, see [Storing credentials for Dependabot to use](/code-security/dependabot/working-with-dependabot/configuring-access-to-private-registries-for-dependabot#storing-credentials-for-dependabot-to-use).

{% data reusables.actions.actions-authentication %} {% data variables.product.prodname_github_apps %} must have the `dependabot_secrets` permission to use these endpoints. Authenticated users must have collaborator access to a repository to create, update, or read secrets.
```

--------------------------------

### Add Assignees using addAssigneesToAssignable

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/copilot/how-tos/use-copilot-agents/coding-agent/create-a-pr.md

Adds {% data variables.product.prodname_copilot_short %} to an existing issue as an assignee, preserving other assignees, using the `addAssigneesToAssignable` mutation.

```APIDOC
## POST /graphql/mutation/addAssigneesToAssignable

### Description
Adds {% data variables.product.prodname_copilot_short %} as an assignee to an issue without removing existing assignees.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/graphql

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **query** (string) - Required - The GraphQL mutation string.
- **variables** (object) - Optional - Variables for the mutation.
  - **input** (object) - Required
    - **assignableId** (string) - Required - The GraphQL ID of the issue.
    - **assigneeIds** (array of strings) - Required - An array containing the Bot ID of {% data variables.product.prodname_copilot_short %}.
    - **agentAssignment** (object) - Optional - Configuration for the Copilot agent.
      - **targetRepositoryId** (string) - Required - The GraphQL ID of the repository.
      - **baseRef** (string) - Required - The base branch name.
      - **customInstructions** (string) - Optional - Custom instructions for the agent.
      - **customAgent** (string) - Optional - Specify a custom agent.
      - **model** (string) - Optional - Specify the model to use.

### Request Example
```shell
gh api graphql -f query='mutation { addAssigneesToAssignable(input: { assignableId: "ISSUE_ID", assigneeIds: ["BOT_ID"], agentAssignment: { targetRepositoryId: "REPOSITORY_ID", baseRef: "main", customInstructions: "Collaborate on this task" } }) { assignable { ... on Issue { id title assignees(first: 10) { nodes { login } } } } } }' -H 'GraphQL-Features: issues_copilot_assignment_api_support,coding_agent_model_selection'
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **data.assignable.id** (string) - The GraphQL ID of the issue.
- **data.assignable.title** (string) - The title of the issue.
- **data.assignable.assignees.nodes** (array) - A list of assignees for the issue.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "data": {
    "assignable": {
      "id": "ISSUE_ID",
      "title": "Example Issue Title",
      "assignees": {
        "nodes": [
          {
            "login": "existing-assignee"
          },
          {
            "login": "copilot-swe-agent"
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Git user information (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/migrations/importing-source-code/using-the-command-line-to-import-source-code/importing-a-team-foundation-version-control-repository.md

This command configures your global Git user name and email address. These credentials will be associated with your commits. Replace 'NAME' and 'EMAIL' with your actual name and email address. This is a prerequisite for pushing to GitHub.

```shell
git config --global user.name "NAME"
git config --global user.email EMAIL
```

--------------------------------

### GraphQL Interface for RepositoryDiscussionAuthor

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/graphql/guides/using-the-graphql-api-for-discussions.md

Defines an interface for entities that can author discussions in a repository, such as Users and Organizations. It includes a field `repositoryDiscussions` to fetch discussions authored by the entity, with options for pagination, filtering by answer status, and ordering.

```graphql
"""
Represents an author of discussions in repositories.
"""
interface RepositoryDiscussionAuthor {
  """
  Discussions this user has started.
  """
  repositoryDiscussions(
    """
    Returns the elements in the list that come after the specified cursor.
    """
    after: String

    """
    Filter discussions to only those that have been answered or not. Defaults to
    including both answered and unanswered discussions.
    """
    answered: Boolean = null

    """
    Returns the elements in the list that come before the specified cursor.
    """
    before: String

    """
    Returns the first _n_ elements from the list.
    """
    first: Int

    """
    Returns the last _n_ elements from the list.
    """
    last: Int

    """
    Ordering options for discussions returned from the connection.
    """
    orderBy: DiscussionOrder = {field: CREATED_AT, direction: DESC}

    """
    Filter discussions to only those in a specific repository.
    """
    repositoryId: ID
  ): DiscussionConnection!
}

```

--------------------------------

### Assign Copilot Licenses via GitHub Actions API (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/copilot/tutorials/roll-out-at-scale/assign-licenses/set-up-self-serve-licenses.md

This snippet demonstrates how to use the Octokit client within a GitHub Actions workflow to assign Copilot licenses to users. It targets the organization-level endpoint for adding users to the Copilot subscription. Ensure you have the necessary authentication and context (organization name, usernames) available in your workflow.

```javascript
const { Octokit } = require("@octokit/action");
const octokit = new Octokit();
const response = await octokit.request('POST /orgs/{org}/copilot/billing/selected_users', {
  org: context.repo.owner,
  selected_usernames: [context.payload.sender.login],
  headers: {
    'X-GitHub-Api-Version': '2022-11-28'
  }
})
```
